key_themes: AI acquisition (Dexibit) and new Accesso Intelligence platform for higher-margin recurring revenue
Strong profit growth (+38% pretax) and efficiency gains (headcount reduction, margin improvement) despite modest revenue
Bullish analyst consensus (Buy ratings, ~100%+ upside to 500-575p targets) vs. weak recent share price momentum
sources_checked: LSE RNS, earnings releases, and Investegate announcements
Analyst consensus (Shore Capital, Deutsche Bank, MarketBeat)
X posts on results and acquisition
summary: Accesso reported solid 2025 results with revenue up 1.8% to $155.1m (stronger like-for-like and recurring revenue growth) and pretax profit up 38%, alongside efficiency improvements and the $12.1m Dexibit AI acquisition to launch a cross-platform intelligence product that should reduce footfall cyclicality. Analysts are enthusiastic about the strategic pivot, maintaining Buy ratings with targets implying over 100% upside from current ~250p levels, and the company confirmed 2026 guidance in line with expectations. The biggest risk is execution on integration/leadership transition (CEO Steve Brown stepping down May 2026), plus macro sensitivity in leisure attractions and geopolitical exposure in the Middle East.

recent_posts.highlight: Accesso harnessing AI in next phase of growth plans, say analysts
